hypothesis: If adaptive fragment- and similarity-based diversity constraints are applied during REINVENT4 reinforcement learning to generate singlet-fission candidate molecules, then the resulting molecule sets will exhibit greater chemical diversity (higher mean pairwise representation distance, more clusters, more unique fragments and scaffolds, and fewer overrepresented SMILES n-grams) than unconstrained generation while maintaining comparable Chemprop-predicted ΔE_SF performance.
